pattern: Precomputing a prefix-sum array once turns every range-sum query into a single subtraction, trading linear-per-query work for constant-time lookups.
hints: The sum over a window can be expressed using two cumulative totals measured from the start of the array.
Build an array where entry i holds the sum of all elements before index i, done once in the constructor.
Then the sum from left to right inclusive is prefix[right + 1] minus prefix[left].

edge_inputs.description: mixed signs with overlapping ranges including the full span
single element queried as a degenerate range
single-index ranges must return that one element, not zero
negatives so a wider range can sum lower than a narrower one

stress.description: moderate array with many random valid ranges
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 10000))
    nums = [rng.randint(-100000, 100000) for _ in range(n)]
    ops = ['NumArray']
    args = [[nums]]
    for _ in range(max(1, size)):
        l = rng.randrange(n)
        r = rng.randrange(l, n)
        ops.append('sumRange')
        args.append([l, r])
    return [ops, args]
stress.note: every range satisfies left <= right < n by construction
